name: Pear'n Cranberry Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cup Cranberries, fresh or frozen, coarsely chopped by hand or food processor
3 tbsp Cranberry juice, orange juice, or water
1 1/2 tsp Cranberry juice, orange juice, or water (actually 1 to 2 teaspoons)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees.
2. Prepare piecrust according to package directions for a two-crust pie using a 9-inch pie pan.
3. In a large bowl, combine pears, cranberries, 1 cup sugar, cornstarch, and 3 tablespoons juice; mix lightly.
4. Spoon the mixture into the piecrust-lined pan and dot with margarine.
5. Unfold the second crust and use a 2-inch cookie cutter to cut out desired shapes in several places.
6. Place the crust over the filling; flute.
7. Brush the underside of the 2-inch cutouts with water and place on top of the crust.
8. Bake at 425 degrees for 40 to 50 minutes, or until golden brown. Cover the edge of the piecrust with strips of foil after 15 to 20 minutes of baking to prevent excessive browning.
9. To make a glaze, blend confectioners' sugar and juice in a small bowl, adding enough juice for the desired consistency. Drizzle over warm pie.
10. Garnish as desired. Makes 8 servings.
